build.notes: Build compiles successfully with only webpack performance warnings (asset size limits). No TypeScript errors.

issues.message: When using BaseControl component, if a label property is passed an id property should also be passed
Usage of __experimentalLinkControl from @wordpress/block-editor is not allowed
issues.notes: This is a known pattern used across the codebase (also in Hero/QuickNavigation). Acceptable as LinkControl is the standard approach for link editing.

issues.message: When using BaseControl component, if a label property is passed an id property should also be passed

footerInfo.notes: Uses revised attributes from DEC-010 (separate campus fields). All defaults match spec.

footerInfoSocialItem.notes: link.opensInNewTab defaults to true as specified

issues.description: In the minimal content variation, FooterNavColumn renders an empty <ul> element when there are no child items. The <ul> contains only whitespace. This is a minor HTML quality issue.
issues.notes: The $children guard may not catch whitespace-only content from WordPress block rendering

issues.scenario: Empty legal links in minimal variation
issues.description: In the minimal content variation, FooterCredits renders an empty <nav> with empty <ul> for legal links when no FooterCreditsItem children exist. Similar to FQA-EDGE-001, $children whitespace check may not be sufficient.

FooterNavColumn has aria-labelledby referencing a non-existent heading ID. theme_block_props() auto-adds aria-labelledby="{blockId}-heading" because the block has a 'heading' attribute, but the <h3> element does not have a matching id="{blockId}-heading" attribute. This makes aria-labelledby point to nothing.
issues.verification: Inspect rendered HTML and confirm aria-labelledby value matches the h3 element id

markupDuplication.notes: No duplicated markup for responsive visibility found. All blocks use single markup with responsive Tailwind classes.

issues.description: FooterInfoSocialItem: TSX renders a <span> as the icon container (line 249), while PHP renders an <a> link element (line 24-34). In the editor, the social item icon is not wrapped in a link - it is a plain <span>. On the frontend, the icon is inside an <a> with href, target, rel, and aria-label. This is an expected TSX/PHP difference for link items (editor uses toolbar link vs frontend anchor), but the TSX is missing the no-underline! and hover:text-blue classes that the PHP <a> has. The editor preview does not match the frontend link styling.
issues.notes: The TSX/PHP difference in element type (<span> vs <a>) is the standard editor pattern for link-based blocks. However, the class divergence (missing no-underline!, hover:text-blue in TSX) means editor preview differs from frontend. This is a minor visual difference in editor only.

issues.description: FooterNav: TSX has an extra <div className="relative"> wrapper around the Appender and inner blocks content (line 33-36), while PHP directly renders the grid div inside <nav>. The PHP output lacks this extra div wrapper. This means the HTML structure differs between editor and frontend, though the visual impact is minimal since the extra div is mainly for Appender positioning.
issues.notes: The extra <div class="relative"> in TSX is for Appender positioning - standard pattern. This is an acceptable editor-only wrapper.

footer.notes: Uses TEMPLATE + REQUIRED + useEffect ordering pattern correctly

footerInfoSocial.notes: Appender for adding social icon items

footerNavColumn.notes: Uses <ul> wrapper for semantic list structure

footerCredits.notes: Uses <ul> wrapper for semantic list structure

issues.description: Social media items in the Full Content variation have no logo/icon uploaded (logoId not set). The spec calls for social media icons (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, YouTube). The links render correctly with sr-only text and aria-labels, but no visible icon content is displayed since no media was uploaded. The links are effectively invisible 24x24 click targets with only screen reader text.
issues.notes: This is a test page content issue rather than a code issue. The social items need actual SVG icon uploads for the icons to be visible.

issues.description: FooterNavColumn heading element missing id attribute that aria-labelledby references. theme_block_props() auto-adds aria-labelledby="{blockId}-heading" but the <h3> has no matching id. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issues.verification: Inspect rendered HTML: the h3 id should match the div aria-labelledby value ({blockId}-heading)

issues.description: FooterInfoSocialItem: TSX uses <span> for icon display (editor), PHP uses <a> for link (frontend). The TSX <span> is missing classes that exist on the PHP <a>: no-underline!, hover:text-blue. While the element type difference is the standard editor pattern for link items, the class mismatch means the editor preview icon size/behavior slightly differs from frontend.

change.to: Acceptable editor/frontend difference for link-based blocks. No action required unless exact editor preview match is needed.

issues.verification: Compare social icon appearance in editor vs frontend

issues.description: FooterNav TSX has extra <div className="relative"> wrapper around Appender + innerBlocksProps that does not exist in PHP output. This is the standard Appender positioning pattern and is acceptable as an editor-only wrapper.
FooterInfo.tsx and FooterInfoSocialItem.tsx: BaseControl components have label property without corresponding id property
issues.verification: Run bun run lint and confirm no BaseControl errors for Footer blocks

issues.description: FooterNavColumn renders empty <ul> when no child items exist. The $children whitespace check does not filter out WordPress block rendering whitespace. Similarly, FooterCredits renders empty <nav>/<ul> for legal links when no FooterCreditsItem children exist.
issues.verification: Load minimal content variation and inspect HTML - empty <ul> and <nav> elements should not render

issues.description: Social media items in Full Content variation render without visible icons because no logoId/media was uploaded. The links work correctly with aria-labels and sr-only text, but the 24x24 click targets have no visual content. Icons need to be uploaded in the editor for social items to be visually functional.
issues.verification: Upload SVG icons for each social media item in the editor and verify they display correctly

issues.description:
FooterNavColumn TSX always renders the subHeading RichText (line 56-63) as a visible element with placeholder text, while PHP conditionally renders it only when subHeading is not empty (line 19-21). In the editor, all columns show a 'Sub-heading...' placeholder even when subHeading is empty. On the frontend, the

is hidden. This is a functional difference: the editor shows extra content that doesn't appear on the frontend. This is the standard shouldDisplay pattern but worth noting for editor/frontend parity.
issues.verification: View a nav column without subHeading in editor - placeholder is visible. View frontend - no subHeading element renders.

issues.description: The footer uses <footer> element correctly for contentinfo landmark. <nav> with aria-label is used for both footer navigation and legal links. <ul>/<li> structure is used for link lists. Social media items use sr-only spans and aria-labels. Dynamic copyright year is generated. All heading levels use h3 consistently within the footer. Overall semantic structure is good.

passedChecks: Build compiles without TypeScript errors
All 9 blocks register correctly with proper block.json configuration
All blocks have required files (block.json, index.tsx, Component.tsx, Component.php, Component.svg)
Block hierarchy correctly enforces parent/child relationships via parent attribute
Footer parent uses <footer> element with multiple:false to prevent duplicates
Footer uses TEMPLATE + REQUIRED + useEffect ordering pattern correctly
All section blocks (FooterInfo, FooterNav, FooterCredits) have inserter:false and multiple:false
All attributes defined with correct types and defaults matching spec
DEC-010 revised attributes (campus1Name, campus1Address, campus2Name, campus2Address) correctly implemented
Social media link opensInNewTab defaults to true as specified
FooterNav uses <nav> with aria-label="Footer Navigation"
FooterCredits legal links use <nav> with aria-label="Legal Links"
FooterNavColumn uses <ul>/<li> structure for semantic link lists
Social items have sr-only spans and aria-labels for screen readers
External links use target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"
Copyright year dynamically generated in both TSX (new Date().getFullYear()) and PHP (date('Y'))
No markup duplication found - all responsive behavior uses Tailwind classes
Test page contains 4 variation instances (Full Content, Minimal, Long Content, Single Campus)
All inner blocks configurations match spec (templates, allowed blocks, appenders)
renameBlock() used correctly on FooterNavColumn, FooterNavColumnItem, FooterCreditsItem, FooterInfoSocialItem
Theme color tokens used throughout - no hardcoded hex values
Background gradient applied correctly via inline style

variations.name: Full Content (Figma Match)
variations.notes: All content populated including addresses, hours, contact, nav columns with items, credits with legal links. Social items present but without uploaded icons.

variations.name: Minimal Content
variations.notes: Labels show with empty content. Single nav column with heading only. No legal links.

variations.name: Long Content
variations.notes: Long address, hours, contact text renders without layout breaking. Long heading text wraps correctly due to overflow-hidden on column.

variations.name: Single Campus
variations.notes: Only campus1 renders. campus2 correctly hidden. Single nav column and single legal link.
